short_text: Wiese to Simi Singh, 3 runs, So, it's another slower ball from Wiese, shorter and Singh steps out, and mistimes it onto
full_text: Wiese to Simi Singh, 3 runs, So, it's another slower ball from Wiese, shorter and Singh steps out, and mistimes it onto the track. This time a tad wider of the bowler and the batters go for the risky single. Wiese still has the time to nail the underarm throw at the striker's end but not only does he miss, but also gets it well wide of the keeper. Allows for the second run and then, a third run is also pinched. That was a clear run out chance but the throw from the keeper's end to the bowler's end is way off-target. So much drama and the Namibia skipper has a wry smile as he sees the funny side of it.

short_text: Wiese to Young, 1 run, shortish and around leg stump, pulled away hard but straight to deep mid-wicket
full_text: Wiese to Young, 1 run, shortish and around leg stump, pulled away hard but straight to deep mid-wicket

short_text: Wiese to Simi Singh, Mark Adair Run Out!! Superb stuff all-round from Wiese! Slower ball, shorter length and Singh despi
full_text: Wiese to Simi Singh, Mark Adair Run Out!! Superb stuff all-round from Wiese! Slower ball, shorter length and Singh despite using his feet can only mistime it onto the track near the bowler. It's the final over, so the batters decide to take a high-risk single. Mark Adair was always going to be in danger with Wiese so close to the ball and the all-rounder makes no mistake with his underarm throw at the keeper's end. Adait was well short.

short_text: Wiese to Mark Adair, 1 run, slower ball on a shortish length, wider line, Adair bottom-edges the cut towards extra cover
full_text: Wiese to Mark Adair, 1 run, slower ball on a shortish length, wider line, Adair bottom-edges the cut towards extra cover

short_text: Wiese to Simi Singh, 1 run, slower ball on a good length, Singh is down the track and plays a punchy-drive through the c
full_text: Wiese to Simi Singh, 1 run, slower ball on a good length, Singh is down the track and plays a punchy-drive through the covers

short_text: Wiese to Mark Adair, 1 run, keeps it really full and on off stump, jammed out down towards long-off. They think of the s
full_text: Wiese to Mark Adair, 1 run, keeps it really full and on off stump, jammed out down towards long-off. They think of the second but decide against it eventually

short_text: Smit to Mark Adair, 1 run, fuller and much wider, Adair slices the drive towards wide long-off, just another single
full_text: Smit to Mark Adair, 1 run, fuller and much wider, Adair slices the drive towards wide long-off, just another single

short_text: Smit to Simi Singh, 1 run, Singh charges down straightaway, gets what all his mates have been getting over the last many
full_text: Smit to Simi Singh, 1 run, Singh charges down straightaway, gets what all his mates have been getting over the last many overs - a slower ball, nurdled down to long-on

short_text: Smit to Neil Rock, Bowled!! Namibia continue to be frugal at the death! The yorker this time, good change up from Smit a
full_text: Smit to Neil Rock, Bowled!! Namibia continue to be frugal at the death! The yorker this time, good change up from Smit and nails this in the blockhole perfectly. Rock was backing away to access the off-side and could only get an under-edge onto the sticks. Ireland are simply unable to get any sort of impetus in these slog overs. They would dearly love to get to at least 130.

short_text: Smit to Mark Adair, 1 run, slower and shorter outside off, punched to extra cover
full_text: Smit to Mark Adair, 1 run, slower and shorter outside off, punched to extra cover

short_text: Smit to Neil Rock, 1 run, back of a length around off, Rock gives the charge but has no pace to play the big shot, adjus
full_text: Smit to Neil Rock, 1 run, back of a length around off, Rock gives the charge but has no pace to play the big shot, adjusts with a push-drive towards long-on

short_text: Smit to Neil Rock, 2 runs, much fuller and outside off, Rock makes room and slices this away behind point. Will get a br
full_text: Smit to Neil Rock, 2 runs, much fuller and outside off, Rock makes room and slices this away behind point. Will get a brace

short_text: Wiese to Neil Rock, 1 run, back of a length slower ball, dabbed towards point
full_text: Wiese to Neil Rock, 1 run, back of a length slower ball, dabbed towards point

short_text: Wiese to Mark Adair, 1 run, angling into the pads, whipped away past square leg
full_text: Wiese to Mark Adair, 1 run, angling into the pads, whipped away past square leg

short_text: Wiese to Harry Tector, Caught by Zane Green!! Wiese has the last laugh! He's too seasoned, too experienced and bowls a c
full_text: Wiese to Harry Tector, Caught by Zane Green!! Wiese has the last laugh! He's too seasoned, too experienced and bowls a cutter that grips a touch off the deck. Tector wanted to cut this away through point but the extra bounce does him in. All he manages is a feather through to the keeper who makes no mistake.

short_text: Wiese to Harry Tector, , precious boundary for Ireland! There is a fine leg fielder and a man at deep square leg, Tector
full_text: Wiese to Harry Tector, , precious boundary for Ireland! There is a fine leg fielder and a man at deep square leg, Tector manages to bisect them with a cross-batted shot. Wasn't entirely in control but the placement did the trick

short_text: Wiese to Neil Rock, 1 run, another pace-off delivery outside off, steered away past point
full_text: Wiese to Neil Rock, 1 run, another pace-off delivery outside off, steered away past point

short_text: Wiese to Harry Tector, 1 run, slower and straighter, bunted down to long-on
full_text: Wiese to Harry Tector, 1 run, slower and straighter, bunted down to long-on

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Curtis Campher, Bowled!! Ireland are imploding a bit here at the back end. Sure, Sharjah these days isn'
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Curtis Campher, Bowled!! Ireland are imploding a bit here at the back end. Sure, Sharjah these days isn't the easiest of decks to accelerate on at the back end but they can definitely better their shot selection. Frylinck once again attacks the sticks, slower cutter and Campher loses his shape while trying to heave this away. Defeated by the lack of pace and the sluggish nature of the deck. Timber!

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Curtis Campher, no run, slower and fuller, Campher makes room early for the slog and misses. Hit on the
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Curtis Campher, no run, slower and fuller, Campher makes room early for the slog and misses. Hit on the back leg

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Harry Tector, 1 run, down the pitch goes Tector and he bunts this back at Frylinck who fumbles to let it
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Harry Tector, 1 run, down the pitch goes Tector and he bunts this back at Frylinck who fumbles to let it through, down to long-off

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Harry Tector, 2 runs, back of a length with some width, punched away into the gap at sweeper cover
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Harry Tector, 2 runs, back of a length with some width, punched away into the gap at sweeper cover

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Harry Tector, no run, short of a length outside off, Tector flashes and misses
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Harry Tector, no run, short of a length outside off, Tector flashes and misses

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, Lbw!! ! Gets rewarded for doing the basics right on this surface - bowls it slow and into the
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, Lbw!! ! Gets rewarded for doing the basics right on this surface - bowls it slow and into the surface. Balbirnie premeditated and wanted to slog sweep but that wasn't the wisest of options on a deck with low bounce. This barely rises and strucks him on the back thigh. On most pitches, this would have gone over the sticks but not at Sharjah. Balbirnie still reviews, and finds Three Reds on ball tracking. Namibia continue their phenomenal fightback.

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Curtis Campher, 2 runs, slower ball dug into the surface, Campher doesn't quite time the pull well
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Curtis Campher, 2 runs, slower ball dug into the surface, Campher doesn't quite time the pull well but gets it into the gap at deep square leg. Easy two by the time deep mid-wicket gets across

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, 1 run, fuller and on off, bunted back to Trumpelmann who has a needless shy at the strik
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, 1 run, fuller and on off, bunted back to Trumpelmann who has a needless shy at the striker's end. Balbirnie was always in his crease and the batters get a run off the ricochet

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, no run, Balbirnie backs away again, Trumpelmann follows him with another slower short ba
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, no run, Balbirnie backs away again, Trumpelmann follows him with another slower short ball, pulled to mid-wicket. Curtis Campher was well down the track and had to go back late.

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, no run, follows Balbirnie who was backing away, cramps him for room and beats him on the
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, no run, follows Balbirnie who was backing away, cramps him for room and beats him on the pull with the slower bouncer

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, 2 runs, fuller and on the pads, chipped over mid-wicket and they scamper back for the se
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Balbirnie, 2 runs, fuller and on the pads, chipped over mid-wicket and they scamper back for the second. The deep fielder hits the bulls-eye at the keeper's end but Balbirnie was way in

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Curtis Campher, 1 run, back of a length outside off, bunted to cover-point and they steal a single
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Curtis Campher, 1 run, back of a length outside off, bunted to cover-point and they steal a single

short_text: Wiese to Balbirnie, no run, yet another dot! Wiese and Namibia really putting on the squeeze. Slower and shorter, short
full_text: Wiese to Balbirnie, no run, yet another dot! Wiese and Namibia really putting on the squeeze. Slower and shorter, short extra cover had moved to the edge of the ring for the final delivery and Balbirnie 's back foot punch is exactly to the fielder

short_text: Wiese to Balbirnie, no run, back of a length around off, Balbirnie stands tall and punches but can't create much impetus
full_text: Wiese to Balbirnie, no run, back of a length around off, Balbirnie stands tall and punches but can't create much impetus on the shot, finds short extra cover again

short_text: Wiese to Balbirnie, no run, more of the slower ones from Wiese, hits the hard length and Balbirnie toe-ends the punch to
full_text: Wiese to Balbirnie, no run, more of the slower ones from Wiese, hits the hard length and Balbirnie toe-ends the punch to short extra cover

short_text: Wiese to Curtis Campher, 1 run, very full and on off stump, jammed out down to long-on
full_text: Wiese to Curtis Campher, 1 run, very full and on off stump, jammed out down to long-on

short_text: Wiese to Gareth Delany, Bowled!! Delany has been put out of his misery! He was struggling to rotate strike against the o
full_text: Wiese to Gareth Delany, Bowled!! Delany has been put out of his misery! He was struggling to rotate strike against the older ball on this slowing surface. Tries to break the shackles with an ugly swipe across the line but this is cleverly bowled by Wiese, slower and away from the slot zone. Stump-to-stump stuff, Delany misses and Wiese hits.

short_text: Wiese to Gareth Delany, no run, slower and shorter around off, nudged to short mid-wicket
full_text: Wiese to Gareth Delany, no run, slower and shorter around off, nudged to short mid-wicket

full_text: Scholtz to Balbirnie, no run, makes room for the inside out loft but the ball barely arrives, mistimed to cover-point

short_text: Scholtz to Gareth Delany, 1 run, speared into the pads, clipped away through backward square leg
full_text: Scholtz to Gareth Delany, 1 run, speared into the pads, clipped away through backward square leg

short_text: Scholtz to Gareth Delany, no run, quicker again, Delany cuts but can't beat point
full_text: Scholtz to Gareth Delany, no run, quicker again, Delany cuts but can't beat point

short_text: Scholtz to Gareth Delany, no run, quicker one around off, keeps bit a low, punched back to the bowler
full_text: Scholtz to Gareth Delany, no run, quicker one around off, keeps bit a low, punched back to the bowler

short_text: Scholtz to Balbirnie, 1 run, sliding into the pads, nurdled behind square leg
full_text: Scholtz to Balbirnie, 1 run, sliding into the pads, nurdled behind square leg

short_text: Scholtz to Balbirnie, , much-needed boundary for Ireland! They have been really strangled post-powerplay and it's been B
full_text: Scholtz to Balbirnie, , much-needed boundary for Ireland! They have been really strangled post-powerplay and it's been Balbirnie who has found the fence twice now in the last few overs. Was a bit too straight in line, he shuffled across and swept wide of short fine leg. Ensuring that it was finer of deep backward square leg

short_text: Smit to Gareth Delany, 2 runs, bit more wider this time and Delany drives it through the point region, easy two
full_text: Smit to Gareth Delany, 2 runs, bit more wider this time and Delany drives it through the point region, easy two

full_text: Smit to Balbirnie, 1 run, fuller and outside off, Balbirnie drives to sweeper cover

full_text: Smit to Balbirnie, no run, back of a length on off, Balbirnie makes room and punches to cover

short_text: Smit to Balbirnie, no run, short and outside off, Balbirnie goes for the cut but the slow nature of the surface does not
full_text: Smit to Balbirnie, no run, short and outside off, Balbirnie goes for the cut but the slow nature of the surface does not allow him to middle it, off the under-edge and on the bounce to Zane Green

full_text: Smit to Gareth Delany, 1 run, angled in from round the wicket, Delany flicks it in front of square on the leg-side

full_text: Smit to Gareth Delany, no run, short of length outside off, Delany steers straight to short third man

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Gareth Delany, 1 run, quicker and skidding on, Delany squirts an inside edge through the mid-wicket region

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, 1 run, goes back to aiming at the pads, Balbirnie clips it to deep mid-wicket

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Gareth Delany, 1 run, quicker and outside off, Delany drives to long-off

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, 1 run, too straight again - on middle and leg, Balbirnie nurdles it through mid-wicket

short_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, , poor delivery and Balbirnie puts it away. Strays down leg and would have been a wide had
full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, , poor delivery and Balbirnie puts it away. Strays down leg and would have been a wide had Balbirnie let go, but he uses the pace and laps it past short fine. Much needed boundary.. First since the Powerplay

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Gareth Delany, 1 run, darted into the pads, Delany flicks to deep mid-wicket

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Gareth Delany, 1 run, fuller and outside off, driven to long-off

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, 1 run, back of a length on off, Balbirnie punches to long-on

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Gareth Delany, 1 run, on middle and leg, Delany works it towards mid-wicket and races across, quick single

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Gareth Delany, , straying down leg, no chance for Delany to connect with the glance

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Gareth Delany, no run, good length ball on off, Delany defends it down the wicket

short_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, no run, appeal for LBW. Aleem Dar is not interested. Erasmus doesn't take the review. Balb
full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, no run, appeal for LBW. Aleem Dar is not interested. Erasmus doesn't take the review. Balbirnie sits down to play the sweep, misses and is struck around the kneeroll. Height might have been an issue

short_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, no run, sliding on and stays low, Balbirnie is hurried into bringing his bat down and he k
full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, no run, sliding on and stays low, Balbirnie is hurried into bringing his bat down and he keeps it out

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, , spinning down leg and Balbirnie cannot connect with the glance

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, no run, fraction short and around off, Balbirnie cracks it towards backward point

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Gareth Delany, leg byes, 1 run, Delany misses the nudge and the ball rolls off the pad to the left of the keeper, Zane Green moves across and shies at the striker's end, misses and Balbirnie survives

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Gareth Delany, no run, slower and at the stumps, Delany turns it to the right of Ya France

full_text: Pikky Ya France to Balbirnie, 1 run, flatter delivery on off, Balbirnie works it through mid-wicket

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, 1 run, fullish and outside off, Balbirnie drives to sweeper cover

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, no run, fuller than a good length, Balbirnie pushes it to cover
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, no run, fuller than a good length, Balbirnie pushes it to cover

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, Caught by van Lingen!! Both openers sent back in successive overs. Namibia are right back
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, Caught by van Lingen!! Both openers sent back in successive overs. Namibia are right back in the contest. This was not a wicket taking delivery. Full and angled into the pads, O Brien flicks it uppishly and picks out van Lingen at deep square leg. He should have placed it better or at least kept it along the ground.

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, no run, on a length and outside off, O Brien toe-ends the drive to cover
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, no run, on a length and outside off, O Brien toe-ends the drive to cover

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, 1 run, fuller and just outside off, Balbirnie drives square and sweeper tidies up

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Balbirnie, no run, slower delivery on off, Balbirnie pushes it back to Frylinck

full_text: Scholtz to Balbirnie, 1 run, stays leg-side, opens the bat face and knocks it to sweeper cover

full_text: Scholtz to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, drifting into the pads, O Brien flicks to deep square leg

full_text: Scholtz to Balbirnie, 1 run, fullish delivery on off, Balbirnie eases it down to long-off

full_text: Scholtz to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, quicker and sliding on, O Brien nudges it through mid-wicket

short_text: Scholtz to Stirling, Caught by Loftie-Eaton!! Scholtz breaks the opening stand. A much needed breakthrough for Namibia i
full_text: Scholtz to Stirling, Caught by Loftie-Eaton!! Scholtz breaks the opening stand. A much needed breakthrough for Namibia in this heat. Stirling used his feet but Scholtz smartly dragged back the length, the Irish opener still goes through with the loft and miscues it towards long-on, Loftie-Eaton runs to his right and slides to complete the catch. Stirling did his job, got Ireland off to a flying start.

short_text: Scholtz to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, flatter and at the stumps, O Brien stays leg-side and punches to long-off
full_text: Scholtz to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, flatter and at the stumps, O Brien stays leg-side and punches to long-off

full_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, six balls, six singles. Fuller than a good length and O Brien drives to sweeper cover

full_text: Smit to Stirling, 1 run, angled into the pads, Stirling works it through the empty mid-wicket region

full_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, once more angled toward the point region and there was a fumble. But the pair don't risk a second

full_text: Smit to Stirling, 1 run, shuffles a long way across and flicks to deep square leg

full_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, on a length and O Brien directs it towards backward point, for a single

full_text: Smit to Stirling, 1 run, change of pace and around off, Stirling punches to long-off

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Stirling, 1 run, on a length and around off, Stirling dabs it towards short third man

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, good length ball on off, O Brien plays it with soft hands in front of cover and crosses over

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, , this is poor bowling. Frylinck once more bowls it right in the slot and O Brien does no
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, , this is poor bowling. Frylinck once more bowls it right in the slot and O Brien does not need a second invitation, he pumps it over mid-off for a one bounce four

short_text: Jan Frylinck to Stirling, 1 run, angled in front of backward point and there's a wayward throw at the keeper's end. No h
full_text: Jan Frylinck to Stirling, 1 run, angled in front of backward point and there's a wayward throw at the keeper's end. No harm done

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, on a length and around off, O Brien directs it square past point

full_text: Jan Frylinck to Kevin O Brien, , O Brien takes a leaf out of Stirling's book. He goes up and over with success. Overpitched around off, O Brien chips it over mid-off and the ball races across the turf

full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, straying onto the pads, flicked to deep mid-wicket

full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, 1 run, slanted into the right hander, Stirling knocks it in front of mid-on and crosses over

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, , uff! That's a uppish backfoot punch. What timing! Wee bit of width and Stirling crunche
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, , uff! That's a uppish backfoot punch. What timing! Wee bit of width and Stirling crunches it over extra cover. Very minimal followthrough of the bat and the ball flies away to the fence

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, 2 runs, slower, dipping full delivery, Stirling is early into the shot and chips it over
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, 2 runs, slower, dipping full delivery, Stirling is early into the shot and chips it over extra cover, just clears the infield and pockets two more

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, no run, back of a length around off, Stirling moves across and tries to mow it across the
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, no run, back of a length around off, Stirling moves across and tries to mow it across the line, misses

short_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, angled in from round the wicket, O Brien tucks it behind square on the leg-si
full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, angled in from round the wicket, O Brien tucks it behind square on the leg-side

short_text: Scholtz to Stirling, , third boundary of the over and 14 runs from it. Stirling goes for the sweep, plants his leg acros
full_text: Scholtz to Stirling, , third boundary of the over and 14 runs from it. Stirling goes for the sweep, plants his leg across and brooms it backward of square

short_text: Scholtz to Stirling, no run, flighted on middle, Stirling prods forward and defends back to Scholtz
full_text: Scholtz to Stirling, no run, flighted on middle, Stirling prods forward and defends back to Scholtz

full_text: Scholtz to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, drifting into the pads, O Brien nurdles it towards square leg

short_text: Scholtz to Stirling, 1 run, quicker and at the stumps, Stirling angles it to backward point and O Brien races across
full_text: Scholtz to Stirling, 1 run, quicker and at the stumps, Stirling angles it to backward point and O Brien races across

short_text: Scholtz to Stirling, , and again.. A carbon copy of the previous shot. Stirling moves away, creates space and goes into
full_text: Scholtz to Stirling, , and again.. A carbon copy of the previous shot. Stirling moves away, creates space and goes into out over cover, this is much flatter and just lands before the boundary rope

short_text: Scholtz to Stirling, , shot! Scholtz is welcomed with a fine hit. Tossed up on middle and leg, Stirling backs away and g
full_text: Scholtz to Stirling, , shot! Scholtz is welcomed with a fine hit. Tossed up on middle and leg, Stirling backs away and goes inside out with the loft, cleared extra cover and almost sent it the distance

short_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, 2 runs, in the air and just short. O Brien makes room and toe-ends the drive, it was uppish and l
full_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, 2 runs, in the air and just short. O Brien makes room and toe-ends the drive, it was uppish and landed in front of cover-point, escapes past the fielder and the pair add two more

short_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, 2 runs, short ball and O Brien waited for the pull, placed it past the diving mid-wicket fielder
full_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, 2 runs, short ball and O Brien waited for the pull, placed it past the diving mid-wicket fielder and scampered back for the second

short_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, no run, angled across off and O Brien uses that to steer to short third man, straight to the fiel
full_text: Smit to Kevin O Brien, no run, angled across off and O Brien uses that to steer to short third man, straight to the fielder

short_text: Smit to Stirling, 1 run, now goes back to the heave across the line, miscued horribly and luckily for him it clears mid-
full_text: Smit to Stirling, 1 run, now goes back to the heave across the line, miscued horribly and luckily for him it clears mid-wicket

short_text: Smit to Stirling, , whoa! He tried to repeat the previous shot but changed at the last instance. Back of a length and ar
full_text: Smit to Stirling, , whoa! He tried to repeat the previous shot but changed at the last instance. Back of a length and around off, Stirling was looking to go over mid-wicket but kept his bat straight to pump it over long-on. Just held his shape and went through the line

short_text: Smit to Stirling, no run, swing and miss. Stirling was aiming for cow corner but the ball does not bounce as much as he
full_text: Smit to Stirling, no run, swing and miss. Stirling was aiming for cow corner but the ball does not bounce as much as he expected, sneaks past his under-edge

short_text: Wiese to Kevin O Brien, no run, fraction short and O Brien is a tad early into the pull, off the toe-end and straight to
full_text: Wiese to Kevin O Brien, no run, fraction short and O Brien is a tad early into the pull, off the toe-end and straight to mid-wicket

full_text: Wiese to Stirling, 1 run, on a length and around off, Stirling dabs it to short third man and is made to hurry by Trumpelmann

short_text: Wiese to Stirling, , in the air and over backward point. Finally some intent from the Irish openers. Stirling backs away
full_text: Wiese to Stirling, , in the air and over backward point. Finally some intent from the Irish openers. Stirling backs away and creates the room to free his arms, slices it over the fielder and collects the first boundary

full_text: Wiese to Stirling, no run, fuller than a good length, Stirling makes a bit of room and drives on the up, straight to cover

full_text: Wiese to Kevin O Brien, 1 run, back of a length angled in, O Brien nurdles it behind square on the leg-side and settles with a single

short_text: Wiese to Kevin O Brien, no run, nips back in and catches O Brien rooted to the crease, the ball beats the inside edge an
full_text: Wiese to Kevin O Brien, no run, nips back in and catches O Brien rooted to the crease, the ball beats the inside edge and strikes him above the kneeroll. Height was the only thing saving O Brien there

full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, no run, good length ball around off, Stirling taps it on the head and directs it towards point

full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, , bit of swing but the wrong line. Starts way down leg and keeps swing further down, needed a full length dive from Zane Green to collect that

full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, no run, much straighter and Stirling gently turns it to mid-wicket

full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Kevin O Brien, no run, fullish delivery close to off, O Brien doesn't move his feet as he blocks to backward point

full_text: Ruben Trumpelmann to Stirling, 1 run, angled across off, Stirling sits back and defends, the ball takes the outer half and rolls down to third man

short_text: Young to Wiese, , samshed over extra cover and that's it. Crazy scenes in the middle, Wiese lets out a loud roar and the
full_text: Young to Wiese, , samshed over extra cover and that's it. Crazy scenes in the middle, Wiese lets out a loud roar and the skipper at the other end is on his knees trying to process what his team has done after that terrible loss in their opening game. The dug-out looks all vibrant and a few players spread across the boundary circumference to display their excitement....stuff of dreams

full_text: Young to Erasmus, 1 run, full and just outside off, Erasmus toe-ends the drive to extra cover

full_text: Young to Erasmus, , up and over extra cover. A Gets under the full delivery and pumps it with superb timing on the off-side, gets a handshake from his partner and a grand round of applause from the dressing room follows...

short_text: Young to Erasmus, no run, I0$ fullish delivery that skids low and has brushed the off-stump before nestling into the kee
full_text: Young to Erasmus, no run, I0$ fullish delivery that skids low and has brushed the off-stump before nestling into the keeper's gloves. There was a huge appeal as the Irish fielders gathered around the stumps to inspect what exactly happened and the bails just stayed intact. There sure looked to be some deflection towards the keeper though

full_text: Young to Wiese, 1 run, goes hard after a short and wide delivery and ends up chopping it into the covers

full_text: Young to Wiese, , David Wiese has killed this chase in a matter of 2 deliveries. Short once again and he's pumped it out of the ground over mid-wicket for a mighty maximum

full_text: Young to Wiese, , the power of David Wiese is just enough to clear the ropes! Dropped short outside off, Wiese muscles the pull to the right of wide long-on and all KOB could do was leap and fingertip it over the ropes

full_text: Curtis Campher to Wiese, 1 run, good length on and around off-stump, a firm punch down to long-on to get off the mark for David Wiese

short_text: Curtis Campher to Zane Green, Caught by Kevin O Brien!! Ah, Kevin O Brien, he's done it again. Green looked to go big st
full_text: Curtis Campher to Zane Green, Caught by Kevin O Brien!! Ah, Kevin O Brien, he's done it again. Green looked to go big straight down the ground and charged out before lifting the full delivery towards mid-off, KOB leapt to his fullest and plucked it out of thin air to complete his second catch. Turns back and has his arms aloft in celebration. Ireland were under a bit of pressure after that expensive last over and this was a much-needed wicket for them.

short_text: Curtis Campher to Zane Green, 2 runs, jumps down the pitch and lofts the full delivery up and over extra cover, huge gap
full_text: Curtis Campher to Zane Green, 2 runs, jumps down the pitch and lofts the full delivery up and over extra cover, huge gap there in the deep and they back for two with ease

short_text: Joshua Little to Erasmus, , this is absolute class from Erasmus! He realized there's no-one behind square on the off-sid
full_text: Joshua Little to Erasmus, , this is absolute class from Erasmus! He realized there's no-one behind square on the off-side and made full use of the width on this length delivery to play the latest of late cuts to send it in the gap between short third and backward point

full_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, 2 runs, pitched up very full on leg stump, dug out towards wide long-on and they race back for the second run.

full_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, no run, fuller and around off, driven firmly towards mid-off

short_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, no run, four dots in a row to start for Campher, just upping the pressure on the batters! Onc
full_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, no run, four dots in a row to start for Campher, just upping the pressure on the batters! Once again the line is straight, shortish length, Erasmus tucks it to mid-wicket

short_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, no run, shorter and on leg stump, punched off the back foot towards mid-on
full_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, no run, shorter and on leg stump, punched off the back foot towards mid-on

short_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, no run, back of a length around off, dabbed towards point
full_text: Curtis Campher to Erasmus, no run, back of a length around off, dabbed towards point

short_text: Curtis Campher to Craig Williams, Caught by Kevin O Brien!! This was hit really hard and was travelling flat over his he
full_text: Curtis Campher to Craig Williams, Caught by Kevin O Brien!! This was hit really hard and was travelling flat over his head. He times his leap well, trekking back a bit from mid-on and pouches this overhead calmly. Excellent piece of fielding and Ireland have been rewarded for some disciplined bowling. Was on a length, there to be put away but Williams probably went a bit too hard on it. As a result, wasn't able to get the desired elevation on the loft, although he still nearly cleared the infield.

short_text: Young to Zane Green, 2 runs, slower ball, leg-lined, tucked away behind square leg and they scamper back for the second
full_text: Young to Zane Green, 2 runs, slower ball, leg-lined, tucked away behind square leg and they scamper back for the second

short_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, slower and shorter around off, Green pulls it hard but Ireland have started off superbly in
full_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, slower and shorter around off, Green pulls it hard but Ireland have started off superbly in the field, saving quite a few already

short_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, the lines from Young continue to be bang on, shortish around off, no room at all and Green
full_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, the lines from Young continue to be bang on, shortish around off, no room at all and Green can only bunt this towards cover. Must say that the southpaw is struggling a bit to find his tempo

short_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, angling across from a middle-and-leg line, extra bounce and Green dabs it gently towards co
full_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, angling across from a middle-and-leg line, extra bounce and Green dabs it gently towards cover

short_text: Young to Zane Green, 2 runs, sprayed on the pads, glanced away to the left of fine leg. Easy brace
full_text: Young to Zane Green, 2 runs, sprayed on the pads, glanced away to the left of fine leg. Easy brace

short_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, loose ball and Green has missed out on it. Wide slower full toss, Green adjusted to the var
full_text: Young to Zane Green, no run, loose ball and Green has missed out on it. Wide slower full toss, Green adjusted to the variation but cut this straight to backward point

short_text: Mark Adair to Zane Green, 1 run, slower ball on the pads, nurdled towards mid-wicket and they pinch a run
full_text: Mark Adair to Zane Green, 1 run, slower ball on the pads, nurdled towards mid-wicket and they pinch a run

short_text: Mark Adair to Zane Green, no run, angling across from a good length, Green stands tall and defends towards point
full_text: Mark Adair to Zane Green, no run, angling across from a good length, Green stands tall and defends towards point

short_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, 1 run, back of a length closer to the off stump, dabbed to point for a tight single
full_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, 1 run, back of a length closer to the off stump, dabbed to point for a tight single

short_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, , finds the fence this time! Short and wide, Williams stands tall and slap-cuts this fierc
full_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, , finds the fence this time! Short and wide, Williams stands tall and slap-cuts this fiercely, beating the diving point fielder to his right. Namibia starting to get a move-on now

short_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, no run, Was a dazzling back foot punch from Williams, timed the pants off it but couldn't
full_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, no run, Was a dazzling back foot punch from Williams, timed the pants off it but couldn't get it past the infield. Was a half-chance, although it would very, very harsh to call this a chance. Stirlo certainly saved a certain boundary

short_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, , crunched! Full and with plenty of width, Williams obliges and bludgeons this over cover.
full_text: Mark Adair to Craig Williams, , crunched! Full and with plenty of width, Williams obliges and bludgeons this over cover. Over the infield easily and it races away to the fence. Fine shot

short_text: Joshua Little to Craig Williams, 1 run, shortish and on the hips, tucked away down to long leg
full_text: Joshua Little to Craig Williams, 1 run, shortish and on the hips, tucked away down to long leg

full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, 1 run, pitched right up, leg-stump line, dug out towards mid-on and they take a sharp run

short_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, , shorter but Little loses his radar for the second time in the over, slips this one down l
full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, , shorter but Little loses his radar for the second time in the over, slips this one down leg

full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, fuller and angling in on off stump, bunted back to the bowler

short_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, short of a length outside off, Green tries to essay the back foot punch on the off-
full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, short of a length outside off, Green tries to essay the back foot punch on the off-side, toe-ends it down the track

short_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, 2 runs, back of a length around leg stump, kept a trifle low, Green adjusted with a straigh
full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, 2 runs, back of a length around leg stump, kept a trifle low, Green adjusted with a straight-faced push through mid-wicket. Sharp running and it gives them a brace

short_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, back of a length around off, no room and Green gets a bottom-edge that just trickle
full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, back of a length around off, no room and Green gets a bottom-edge that just trickles past the sticks towards the keeper

full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, , very full and sliding down the leg-side, Green looks to glance it away, misses

short_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, angling in from a shorter length, squares up Williams a bit as he dabs this towards poi
full_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, angling in from a shorter length, squares up Williams a bit as he dabs this towards point. Mix up in the running but no harm done in the end

short_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, shorter and closer to off stump, jabbed to the off-side
full_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, shorter and closer to off stump, jabbed to the off-side

short_text: Young to Craig Williams, , smashed down the ground! Intent from Williams, charges down and knows that it's going to be a
full_text: Young to Craig Williams, , smashed down the ground! Intent from Williams, charges down and knows that it's going to be a good length ball. Lofts it straight back over the bowler's head - safe shot and gets the reward for it

short_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, stump-to-stump line again, back of a length, defended down the track
full_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, stump-to-stump line again, back of a length, defended down the track

short_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, short of a length around off, tight line, Williams punches off the back foot but can't
full_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, short of a length around off, tight line, Williams punches off the back foot but can't pierce the off-side gap once more. This is a very tidy start from Ireland

short_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, angling in from a good length,  punchy defence to cover
full_text: Young to Craig Williams, no run, angling in from a good length,  punchy defence to cover

short_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, very full and straight, in-angler, Green misses the flick and is struck on the pads
full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, very full and straight, in-angler, Green misses the flick and is struck on the pads. Looked a decent shout in real time, maybe just going down leg with the angle

short_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, fourth stump line, shaping away a tad, Green fishes at it and is beaten on the outs
full_text: Joshua Little to Zane Green, no run, fourth stump line, shaping away a tad, Green fishes at it and is beaten on the outside edge

short_text: Joshua Little to Craig Williams, 1 run, quite full and very wide, Williams stretches out and slices it over backward poi
full_text: Joshua Little to Craig Williams, 1 run, quite full and very wide, Williams stretches out and slices it over backward point. Third man gets around quickly to keep the batters to just a single

full_text: Joshua Little to Craig Williams, no run, back of a length outside off, extra bounce, steered to point

full_text: Joshua Little to Craig Williams, no run, fuller and around off, push-driven to short cover
